---
title: "Academics and Teaching"
---

<br>

```{r out.width = "50%", fig.align = "center", echo = FALSE}
#knitr::include_graphics("Convocation.jpg")
```

<br>

Here is an overview of my academic career and teaching activities. For more details on these, please take a look at my [CV](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1tdVsxROy1tzUf_wX3RBBIlLnI0lg2Gi2m8y3VlBG380).

## Education

- **PhD Biostatistics**, University of Michigan, 2016 - 2022

Dissertation “Causal Inference Methods and Intermediate Endpoints in Randomized Clinical Trials”

Advisors: Drs. Jeremy Taylor and Michael Elliott

- **MS Biostatistics**, University of Michigan, 2018

- **Bachelor of Arts, Mathematics and Psychology**, Coe College, Cedar Rapids, Iowa, 2016

Summa Cum Laude 

GPA 3.99, Mathematics Departmental Distinction, Scholar’s Program, 
Honors thesis “Roommate Matching by Design” Advisor: Dr. Jonathan White

## Teaching

I will be developing and teaching a graduate-level causal inference course in 2023.

## Graduate Fellowships

University of Michigan Rackham Predoctoral Fellowship, 2021-2022

National Science Foundation Graduate Research Fellowship Program, 2016-2021

University of Michigan School of Public Health Regents’ Fellowship Award, 2016

R.J. McElroy Fellowship, 2016

## Research Experience: Graduate

University of Michigan, Ann Arbor, Michigan	

National Institutes of Health Cancer Training in Biostatistics Grant Trainee, Sept 2016-Aug 2017

Advisor: Dr. Jeremy Taylor

Graduate Student Research Assistant Advisor: Dr. Philip Boonstra, May-Aug 2019

Advisor: Dr. Lili Zhao, 2019, Sept 2020-2022

Advisor: Dr. Matt Schipper, June 2020-2022

Co-PI Telomere Research Network Pilot Project Grant under Dr. Belinda Needham, Aug 2020-2022

Research Experience: Undergraduate

Harvard Summer Program in Biostatistics and Computational Biology Boston, Massachusetts, June-July 2015

Action Research: Mission of Hope Cedar Rapids, Iowa, Jan-May 2015

Columbia Summer Institute in Biostatistics New York City, New York, May-July 2014

Coe College Research in Mathematics Program Cedar Rapids, Iowa, June-Aug 2013

## Awards and Distinctions

University of Michigan Department of Biostatistics Excellence in Research Honorable Mention 2021

School of Public Health Unsung Hero Award 2020	

Barry Goldwater Honorable Mention

Dean’s List Coe College				

Coe Hunt Award in Mathematics	

Honors Societies: Phi Beta Kappa, Mortar Board, Psi Chi, Alpha Lambda Delta, Phi Kappa Phi

## Grants and Awarded Funding 

NIH U24 AG066528-01: Telomere Research Network, Telomere Research Network Analysis Award, 2020-2021, $4,000, (PI: Stacy Drury). Role: Co-PI on project exploring mediation of the Black/White telomere length difference by persistent organic pollutant exposure.

Rackham Graduate Travel Grant 2018, 2019, 2021, 2022, Michigan Institute of Data Science Travel Grant 2019, SAMSI Travel Grant 2019, Telomere Network Analysis Award and Travel Grant 2020, 2021

## Society Membership

American Statistical Association, ENAR, Society for Clinical Trials, Caucus for Women in Statistics
